title: Physiotherapist (NUHS@Home)(7391)
description:
Join NUHS@Home, an innovative and transformative healthcare initiative at the National University Health System. We are looking for a forward-thinking Physiotherapist to play a key role in revolutionising patient care within our Hospital-at-Home program. This flexible role, available as full or part-time, allows you to practice at the top of your license, leveraging your expertise in physiotherapy to make an impact on patient outcomes.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Assess and manage patients referred for physiotherapy independently and implement appropriate treatment plans.
  • Report to referring doctor and other members of the medical team.
  • Document patient physiotherapy records accurately.
  • Charge patients accurately.
  • Comply with clinical and service standards.
  • Practise within areas of professional competence and seek clinical support from supervisor / other healthcare professionals when appropriate.
  • Collaborate with other healthcare members and participate in case conferences.
  • Assist and/or co-lead in projects and activities which supports the goals of the department and organisation at department or institution level.
  • Any other duties or responsibilities as assigned.
  • Manage and oversee therapy equipment inventory.
  • Contribute to departmental in-service training programmes, home hospital educational talks and external talks/ workshops/ forums/ seminars
  • Contribute to development and implementation of protocols and guidelines that will shape the future of hospital-at-home care at NUHS
  • Review and update of clinical protocols and SOPs
  • Assist in developing, promoting /marketing clinical services
  • Assist in the induction of new physiotherapists.
  • Supervise students on clinical attachment
  • Embrace continuous learning and professional development opportunities.

Requirements:

  • Full AHPC registration
  • Recognised Diploma or Degree in Physiotherapy
  • Min. 2 years of relevant experience
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills
  • Service oriented and customer focus
  • Motivated, self-driven and able to work independently and collaboratively in a team
  • Passion for transforming traditional healthcare models through innovative care delivery

postedCompany.description:
The National University Health System (NUHS) plays a key, contributing role in Singapore’s healthcare. Our versatile and experienced team of healthcare professionals has only one aim - to help people get better, and live healthier and longer lives.

We do this by improving medical care, lowering disease recurrence, producing better healthcare professionals, and turning research findings into better medical treatments for diseases that affect Singaporeans. And the reason we’re able to do these things is because we have the best and most dedicated people. Come join us and help shape medicine for our future.
postedCompany.name: NATIONAL UNIVERSITY HEALTH SYSTEM PTE. LTD.
